nodes.label: AI as everyday life assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI as untrustworthy or irrelevant for personal queries
nodes.grounding_span: I typically use AI most when I'm around the house to help questions that come up

nodes.label: AI integration in healthcare administrative tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI integration hindered by privacy and system fragmentation
nodes.grounding_span: I would love to just spend my time with the client/patient rather than with the overhead of documentation, appointment scheduling, and billing

nodes.label: AI handling of sensitive health information
nodes.label_negative: AI lacking HIPAA compliance and security
nodes.grounding_span: HIPAA is a big concern in our healthcare space - we definitely need to keep client/patient information secure and private

nodes.label: AI as replacement for human expertise and connection
nodes.label_negative: AI as complementary tool preserving human role
nodes.grounding_span: I don't see my particular role being replaced by AI anytime soon

nodes.label: Digitization of healthcare workflows enabling AI
nodes.grounding_span: It would be nice to have everything digitized already

nodes.label: Efficiency and reduced administrative burden

nodes.label: Human connection and personalized care
nodes.grounding_span: There is no AI that can replace that human connection and deep care

nodes.grounding_span: I really use it instead of a search engine like I used to

nodes.label: Routine-based workflow with manual processes
nodes.grounding_span: I do have the workflow/process all written out and I do it often, so it has become routine
